post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 155: Memetrix6 Mafia!
IF YOU ARE NOT CURRENTLY IN THIS GAME, DO NOT POST
Credits to Binomial-theorem/chaotic_iak/willwang123 (among others) for the rule template.

Story
[flavor coming soon]
GET MEMED ON
Jk.
Once upon a time, there was a memer. This memer created 9 memes, each with their own personality. Some of them were vanilla people with little personality quirks. Some of them were given special roles to help serve the people. Some of them were given power. However, those with power decided to use their power for bad purposes. They were tired of sharing dankness around the village of memes, and conspired to be the only two memes remaining. Furthermore, their contempt for their maker burned like the contempt of active players for summitwei's modding in res.
At first, the village was unaware of this. But one fateful morning, the village woke up to a horrible sight: their creator and his apprentice, dead. Hung in the middle of the street, with a sign posted above, reading “HERE LIES THOSE WHO WERE NOT DANK ENOUGH. THEY DIED FROM AIDS. REMEMBER AIDS IS BAD FOR YOU. U R NEXT”.
SFGiantsFan was hung. He was The Great Memer (The Mod)
Madelyn was hung. She was The Memer's Apprentice (The Co-mod)


Rules
  • Read all the rules. Including this one.
  • Follow all the rules. Including this one.
  • Read the entire OP.
  • Do not post at night.
  • You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
  • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me, including role PMs. Do not play around with exploiting this rule.
  • Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the moderator in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
  • You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others.
  • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
  • Do not pretend to edit or otherwise pretend to break a rule.
  • If you are dead, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.


Voting/Lynches
  • Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". Playernames must be spelled correctly. If you try to conceal a fakevote and a mod or co-mod does not catch it, it will be counted as a real vote for any purposes. For example, "Vote: fdas" is an acceptable vote, while "Vote: fdas", "Vote fdas", "vote: fdas", "Vote:fdas", "Vote: fads", "Vote: fdsa", "Vote: Fdas" and "Vote: fdas" are not. The entire thing must be bolded. For example:
    Quote:
    Vote: SFGiantsFan
    is acceptable.
    Quote:
    Shoot: SFGiantsFan

    Hope this works
    is not.
  • Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
  • Improperly made votes (including votes in hide tags or any of the unacceptable forms above) will not be counted under any circumstances. I will not tell you if your vote is invalid. If an invalid vote is counted by the mod as a valid vote, move on, it counts.
  • You may vote for no lynch to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Lynch".
  • You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any..
  • If at any point in a day more than half of the players currently alive are voting for a single player, that player will be lynched. It will then immediately be twilight.
  • If at any point in a day at least half of the players alive are voting for no lynch, then there will be no lynch. It will then be twilight.
  • The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
  • During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
  • Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person lynched (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.


Activity
  • Each day will be 336 hours (2 weeks). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
  • The deadline will occur the very second 336 hours has occurred.
  • If the deadline is met and no person is lynched, it will be considered no lynch and will proceed to be twilight.
  • Each night will be 3 real-life days long.
  • When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
  • Any night results will be sent after the night deadline.
  • Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night and start of the next day.
  • If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive an in-game prod.
  • If a player does not post in another 36-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
  • If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless he/she posts before the moderator finds a replacement.
  • If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long. If your V/LA will be longer than about a week, please either try to find a way to participate in the game during that time or repuest reqlacement.


Etiquette
  • Do not use inappropriate language.
  • Do not make any personal attacks.
  • Be mature.
  • For a minor rule infraction, newbies can receive up to two warnings, and SE/IC’s up to one. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled. Ask not to be modkilled if you’ve read these rules. Please do not make me modkill you.


Moderators
  • I (SFGiantsFan) am the Moderator of Game 155. Madelyn is the co-moderator (and hopefully not the incompetent toedmod) of Game 155.
  • Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
  • Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
  • Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
  • Please do not post in those colors or in any shades of those colors.
  • The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.


Setup
This game will use the Matrix6 setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Three of the roles will be generated randomly by picking a row or column from the matrix. Then one Mafia Goon and 5 Vanilla Townies are added to maintain a 2:7 mafia:town ratio. The six possible setups are:
1. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
2. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 2 Mafia Goons
3. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons
4. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 2 Mafia Goons
5. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Mafia Goon, 1 Mafia Roleblocker
6. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons

Note that if applicable, a Mafia Roleblocker's action takes precedence over a Town Jailkeeper's action. The power of the 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie cannot be roleblocked.


Role PMs
Where. Is. Your. Evidence. (Town Tracker) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],


You are Where. Is. Your. Evidence. (Town Tracker). You are infatuated with knowing other people’s evidence. Once a night, you may PM me one person's name. You will see the evidence of who that person targeted each night, if any. The thread can be found here.

You win when all Mafia are killed.
BOOOOONNNNNNNGGGGGGG (1-Shot Bulletproof Townie) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],


You are BOOOOONNNNNNNGGGGGGG (1-Shot Bulletproof Townie). You have a bulletproof vest, and any bullets directed towards you just bounce off of it, making a loud BOOOOONNNNNNNGGGGGGG. The first time a mafioso attempts to NK you, you will still be alive the next day. Your power is reflexive and cannot be roleblocked. The thread can be found here.

You win when all Mafia are killed.
Descended into Incoherence (Town Jailkeeper) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],


You are Descended into Incoherence (Town Jailkeeper). For the safety of the village, if you feel someone has descended into incoherence, you lock them up for the night. Once a night, you may PM me one person's name. You will roleblock them, preventing them from using their night action, but you will also protect them. The thread can be found here.

You win when all Mafia are killed.
as facebook (Town Cop) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],


You are as facebook (Town Cop). You’re really good at determining people’s alignment from their facebook profiles. Once a night, you may PM me one person's name. You will then know if they are town or antitown (or if you failed to receive results) via their facebook profile. The thread can be found here.

You win when all Mafia are killed.
hmmm (Town Doctor) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],


You are hmmm (Town Doctor). You’re a doctor. Doctors spend a lot of time going “hmmm”, whether when in school trying to get their degree or thinking about how to heal a patient. Once a night, you may PM me a person's username. That person will then be protected from getting killed that night. The thread can be found here.

You win when all Mafia are killed.
flavor coming soon (Vanilla Townie) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],


You are [flavor coming soon] (Vanilla Townie). Your special abilities are just like your flavor, coming soonTM. Until then, your strength comes in your voice and your vote. The thread can be found here.

You win when all Mafia are killed.
for obvious reasons (Mafia Goon) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],


You are a for obvious reasons (Mafia Goon). For obvious reasons, you’re a mafia goon. You and your partner, [username], [role name], are out to kill all other memes in the village. During the night, you may discuss with your partner in [this quicktopic]. The thread can be found here.

You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or nothing can prevent it.
Defenestration (Mafia Roleblocker) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],


You are Defenestration (Mafia Roleblocker). You like to defenestrate people in the twilight, knocking them out for the night and rendering them useless. With your partner, [username], for obvious reasons (Mafia Goon), you may kill somebody every night. In addition, you can defenestrate someone, keeping them from performing any night actions. During the night, you may discuss with your partner in [this quicktopic]. The thread can be found here.

You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or nothing can prevent it.


Alive (5)
  • jonyj1005 summitwei
  • jeffisepic
  • benjaminsz
  • goodbye chess24
  • tikkitamatoa


Dead (4)
  • e_is_cool, (flavor coming soon) (Vanilla Townie) was stoned to death D1.
  • The excitement of the game died of natural causes N2
  • happycupcake, BOOOOOONGGGGGGGG (1-Shot Bulletproof Townie) was thrown off a cliff D3
  • PCampbell, Descended into Incoherence (Town Jailkeeper) was crushed by a fiery fist N3
  • hdgehrke, (flavor coming soon) (Vanillia Townie) was mauled by an Ugandan Knuckles D4.


Significant Events
e_is_cool is lynched D1
No one dies N1
No one is lynched D2
No one dies N2
happycupcake is lynched D3
PCampbell dies N3
hdgehrke is lynched D4


Warnings
3 warnings and you will be modkilled or force-replaced
SE/ICs only get 2 warnings, for obvious reasons.

tikkitamatoa receives a warning for OOT

Do not post until this line is crossed out, unless you are Madelyn or SFGiantsFan knock yourselves out
This game may not start for a day or so. Be patient.
is that a meme yet

Congratulations to the scumteam, jeffisepic (Mafia Goon) and tikkitamatoa (Mafia Roleblocker) for winning Mafia Game 155!

post_rendered:
Gold flavor by the way. Here's a little icebreaker to get us started:

How is your playstyle different from town and villager? Which do you find more enjoyable? If this is your first game then introduce yourself or something.

I personally find my mafia play much more careful, while as town I just post whatever is on my mind. As mafia I always double check what I'm typing before pressing submit to think whether what I'm typing will be towny or not. I definitely find mafia more enjoyable because I'm usually more concise.

As a villager I 180 my reads a lot. I am usually proactive and evaluate people's reactions under pressure. I also notice that I tunnel people and do micro-reads a lot which is a habit I'm trying to break.

post_rendered:
I have listed benjaminsz's arguments in bold and my rebuttals in non-bold.
Firstly, the phrase "I think town or scum would want to lay low on their first game" is utterly false. Anyone with experience will tell you that newbscum are far more likely to lay low than newbtown. I still don't think you're right. I think for a townie's first game, it depends on personality more than anything. You're right, some people will want to rush into the game and start presenting their ideas, but I know I wasn't eager to do that at the start of this game. This is a new environment for everyone, and at the start it doesn't seem very accepting to beginners. Make an unclear joke, use the wrong tone, wrongly accuse someone, or make just one of a number of other possible mistakes and you're dead. Some people will tread more carefully, and some will dive in.
Secondly, the fear of making mistakes can appear on either side, but is far more common in newbscum, since most newbtown tend to view experienced players as infallible and assume they will townread them (hence why newbtowns might tend to spy a player just for being experienced and reading them wrong). Where are you getting this information and what makes you believe that most new townies will behave in the same way? Also, you might say that I am doing the same thing in these arguments against you. I am just making the case that happycupcake may be an outlier from your model, and it may not be as common as you think.
Thirdly, there is no "jumping to conclusions" involved. If I said happycupcake was guaranteed to be scum, then you'd have a point. I didn't. I just think happycupcake is scummy. It was jumping to a conclusion when you voted for happycupcake.
And just because you (which by "we" you mean 2 people both of whom have no experience) agreed that happycupcake was making a mistake doesn't mean that happycupcake did. Okay, I shouldn't have said that because we didn't have a full group concensus. You're right.
You can forgive newbs for making mistakes, but that's a slippery slope towards letting newbscums win easily - if I could remember things I would cite the game where a newbscum soloed the last three or four town. It's not a slippery slope. If a newb town says something stupid that they later realize they shouldn't have said, they can come forward with that and we understand that they're a newb so it's okay. This gives us the reason why they said it and therefore evidence against them being scum. But if we have evidence that they are scum, then we will lynch them (just as we will lynch anyone with enough evidence towards them being scum.) I don't think cutting them a bit of slack has anything to do with letting them win.
In addition, I'd like to know what you mean by " it's scummy for you to vote for happycupcake this quickly". We've been out of RVS for a long time; voting for anyone you think is scummy is now fair game. Firstly: I have no idea what being out of RVS has to do with this; I never said we were in RVS. The reason I say it's scummy is because you have such a small amount of evidence against happycupcake, and yet you appear eager to lynch them which in my eyes makes you look scummy. I would vote for you, but tikki is right: it's risky to lynch someone on day 1 without a whole lot of evidence.

Gosh, this is a strange situation.

We got Goodbye popping in and voting Ben immediately, happycupcake talking (yay!), defending and attacking, and a couple busy people popping up occasionally to check in.

My views. Ben’s “test” was really kinda dumb, but I think it worked. At least in terms of happycupcake posting more and giving us info. I don’t think they were unreasonably defensive, though. It makes sense. If you’re attacked, you defend. They just seem like a ~towny player who doesn’t really know how to react to all this stuff (no offense...), but they aren’t totally clear in my books either.

I don’t agree with the philosophy that we should vote for people who we think are scummy. I think we should vote for the people who we think are the scummiest, once we have enough evidence. post_rendered:
what frustrates me the most about mafia is that it's so hard to learn. I have put so much time into watching other people play, to learn what the meta is and to learn good logic. When I deploy that logic, people tell me it is bad. I just wish someone would explain to me why my logic is bad, for once, instead of simply dismissing it. As is, people just scumread me and move on, which makes it impossible for me to improve as either alignment, no matter how hard I try. I just wish people would attempt to help me improve beyond my first game. I feel like, ever since and even during my first game, I have no longer received the benefit of the doubt that comes with being a newbie, but have still been dismissed as a newbie with bad logic. While jeff, jony, and e try to decide whether I'm scum or I just have bad logic, I never learn why my logic is bad, and I never improve. I've played 5 games now and am no closer to being a good mafia player than I was after my first game - almost everything I've learned, I've learned on my own, and as should be clear, that has been pitifully insufficient thus far. I feel like no matter how many games I play, I just don't get any better, and no one makes any effort to help me escape this hole. I've seen players who started several games after myself leapfrog me in the rankings, becoming respected high-level newbies (charge) and even SE's (jeff) while I'm still given the lack of respect afforded to first timers. Don't get me wrong, I don't want to point any fingers, or even disparage the community as a whole. But I just don't understand why I'm failing to improve and failing to gain help of any kind. I just don't know if I'll ever be confident in my abilities to play this game effectively, and I feel like I could play until I died and still be little better than I was in that benighted Game 137.

OK emotional outburst over, back to gameplay, sorry about that... *dies inside* *becomes depressed* *decides to quit mafia* *decides that would be unnescesarilly mean to a group of people that certainly did not consciously put me into the situation I'm in* *decides to stay* *forces down internal rage and returns to normal life* *goes to sleep and tries to think about anything, ANYTHING but mafia for a few hours at least*

Dam that's a pure post.

Here's a tip: overusing the words "confbiased, omgus, wifom, bandwagon" is bad logic. If you are just using those words, you will sound like a desperate people searching for an attack. Instead, you should explain why this certain scenario pings you. "Omgus" is a terrible reason to scumread someone. "This person voted me but doesn't say why" is another story. The key part is to ask yourself if a town or wolf can say this. You would find that an answer for why you are town/scumreading someone comes way more natural.

So here's my logic.

e could easily have town motivation. And I won't deny that it would be bad if he was mislynched. But he's been doing some things that you just don't do as town. The town motivation is there, but the scum motivation is much more obvious - he could just be pushing for a mislynch of me. And he's experienced enough to come up with a good fake town motivation as scum to cover his tracks. Most importantly, even if he isn't 100% scum, it doesn't matter; everyone starts with a 2/9 chance to be scum, and no lynch would be pretty bad, which means that if you believe there's any greater than a 2/9 chance of him being scum even if he is more likely town that scum - he's still the best lynch. Now, I'm not advocating the use of math to determine a lynch, I'm just making a point that even if someone appears more town than scum, you can still lynch them if they appear more scum than anyone else. And I believe that e does.

That's my closing argument for this little war. I've defended every attack I can find, and despite what e says, I have explained all of my logic. I'm done with the constant back and forth. It's time for the players in this game to make the decision. I hope they choose wisely.

Oh, and P.S. if tikki switches to voting for me that's a strong scumtell - the exact same type of thing happened in XXVII, only with archimedes, and he was scum. (By that I mean that he was agreeing with me, and then as soon as it looked like e was winning the argument, he agreed with e - he actually had us both pocketed.)

post_rendered: Can someone tell me what “OMGUS” and “pocket” mean?
post_canonical: Can someone tell me what “OMGUS” and “pocket” mean?

post_rendered:
PCampbell wrote:
Can someone tell me what “OMGUS” and “pocket” mean?

OMGUS stands for Oh My God You Suck, and it basically means voting for or attacking someone because they voted for or attacked you. Now keep in mind that if x person attacks y and then y person attacks x, it is not necessarily OMGUS. It is OMGUS if y person attacks x BECAUSE x person attacked y. OMGUS is generally scummy, but not always. It depends on the situation.

To pocket someone is to be nice to them and townread them in order to influence them into town reading you and agreeing with you. Almost always a scum tactic, but some people *cough* summit *cough* will claim they can do it as town.

post_rendered: WIFOM is circular logic. Its most common use is when a spy says "but if I was a spy, I wouldn't do x thing, because I haven't done it in my past spy games", which is obviously wrong since they could be actively avoiding things they've done in past spy games in order to appear as town. So when Jeff says "I don't make my buddying this obvious as scum", he could be doing so to appear town because he hasn't done so in the past, and thus it's WIFOM.
post_canonical: WIFOM is circular logic. Its most common use is when a spy says "but if I was a spy, I wouldn't do x thing, because I haven't done it in my past spy games", which is obviously wrong since they could be actively avoiding things they've done in past spy games in order to appear as town. So when Jeff says "I don't make my buddying this obvious as scum", he could be doing so to appear town because he hasn't done so in the past, and thus it's WIFOM.

post_rendered:
PCampbell wrote:
Are you saying that any meta defense is WIFOM? Because it sounds like that argument could be made towards any meta defense.

Yes, I am. Meta defenses can be used to show that a meta attack is NAI, or Not An Indicator, since the meta attack says "you are doing x, and you only do x as scum" and the meta defense says "i actually don't do x as scum" or "I also do x as town", but using meta to make yourself towny, or using a meta defense to defend against a non-meta attack, is WIFOM and wrong.

post_rendered:
My friends.. I have been enjoying this game a lot, but sadly it is that time of year. I would be way more invested in this game usually.

I have a couple things to say about this game, and my defense littered in there is up for you to decide.


[hide=Bandwagoning[/hide]
Now when is bandwagoning scummy, and when is it not?

Bandwagoning is voting a person that is being voted. Are people not allowed to vote people who they think are scummy? Are people not allowed to agree with someone else? It seems as though you guys got influenced by Benjamin a little too much- Bandwagon is not a buzzword and will only come off as an excuse if you can't find someone scummy. If one is content with what someone else says about another, then it is perfectly reasonable to vote them. Also note that bandwagoning is not scummy; it is the definition. It is the context one will have the evaluate.

When is bandwagoning scummy?
Let me introduce a concept: progression. An unreasonable progression is when a person flips flops on reads to fit the circumstance. For example, a person has been defending person A the whole game, and at the last minute switched the vote to him- a mislynch.

All in all, BW is a term to represent a person voting on a wagon. Bandwagon is not a term that means scummy.[/hide]

[hide=meta and wifom[/hide]
Meta is an interesting one, and I am kind of angered by the fact that he is arrogantly belittling my knowledge because of what he thinks is right, because man it is totally off. Of course, you can take your sides, but I am 90% sure an experienced player will side with me.

Meta is not NAI. Take atomicpianowitch, for example. He is notorious for bussing. After he flips wolf, town must make sure to not give who attack as much credit as for say, I flip wolf. If we observe that jonyj1005 is much more quiet as scum, then if he is quiet this game we can pin him for that. Here is the most important key: Mafia is a team game. Town must work together. If all town are fighting different ways we cannot win. It is up to the majority to decide who is best. Meta can only be one part of an argument, but it is still something. If one person scumreads me for posting too much, it is impossible to refute that other than using meta.

WIFOM is a term you should never use. WIFOM does hold a lot of the time, but the real consideration of how much of it is up to every individual. the word WIFOM in not way can negate a whole argument, and that is by far the biggest mistake I see on this forums, it really is quite frustrating. If the case itself is WIFOM, that can be derived from common sense. If there is logic to it, it is still WIFOM, but it still holds. For example, I can say "Nike is scummy because she is lurking" and Nike can reply using one word: "WIFOM"
Is my statement WIFOM? Of course, but it still holds.[/hide]

Okay look; saying one's attack is something you may think, and you may say. By bias it is natural to say that an attacker says the attack is good and the defendant says the attack is bad. Obviously, the defendant will be adamant in their defense, and try to repetitively yell "this attack is bad" (and is quite annoying). The reason Mafia works on how it is structured is because town is the majority, and that is what makes it so fun. In the me vs ben thing, some scumread me, some scumread ben.

Naturally, that would be scummy, because it would seem I am hiding in the shadows. If I were mafia, I could say "I am very busy." It is a reasonable defense, but it is up to town to decide whether it is credible or not. I can't emphasize that point enough. The attacker/defendant aren't the one who will determine anything, so spamming "I refuted his attack" 500 times will not help, and will hurt play quality. That is an aspect I have to work on, too.

Summit/cupcake seems very likely at this point. There isn’t much to say that hasn’t already been said, but basically I think that summit/cupcake is by far the most likely and logical scum team. More later. Busy this week.

chess24 wrote:
Here's the issue. You were able to say that ben was town and summit was town (implying that you/ben and you/summit were all v/v) even after all of that arguing. But all it took was for ben to say that one little thing, and you said ben/summit could be w/v. I can see lots of scum motivation here; you want to make sure that you're not in any v/w interaction and you want to put two town in a v/v interaction. Saying that you and someone else are v/v after a big fight is not scummy, but when you combine that with starting v/w from just one little thing, I have a hard time finding town motivation in that. Maybe you/ben is v/w. Maybe you/summit is v/w. And maybe ben/summit is v/v. And maybe you're scum.

Many of your posts have some scum motivation. However, most of them have more town than scum motivation. But this post doesn't, and then all the other scum motivation comes out.
No, this is really towny, and I'll explain why:
My style of play here on aops is to put people under reactions and analyze their response. In both these situations I was winning my argument, and I could have totally not backed down and drove their lynch, and probably succeed. Instead what did I do? I townread them. The wolf wincon is to mislynch villagers. By clearing two wolfy villagers, I actually am trying to solve the game. A wolf's optimal line of play is to shade as many villagers as possible, and that is what YOU are doing.
What is your point you are trying to convey? Me saying "I wouldn't be surprised that ben is actually wolf?" I haven't even touched on that, nor have I wanted to consider that slot until lylo. You seeing me change my reads reflects my town meta. It is apparent that you don't believe this though.
chess24 wrote:
Also, since you gave a crazy analogy to happycupcake (which basically says that his case is less likely; however, his case is definitely possible), I'll give you a crazy analogy to show what you're doing.
Suppose at the beginning of the game (or in this case when I replaced in) I said "if I seem scummy, then I am town." Then I cited this later to try to show I'm town. This is pretty close to what you're doing.
Overall this post is also pretty scummy.
wrong wrong wrong. I am not sure you understand meta at all.

Alright, let's get this game rolling and catch some wolves.

First I want to say that this game is very challenging for me, both the wolves and the villagers are playing really well. By far the best newbie game in a long time. Shoutout to tikki and chess24 for making this game so enjoyable.

Obviously, tryhards can be wolf. I was tryhard in my first game (D3+), and I won't silly clear anyone out of effort especially as it is D3.
but let's do this, solve this game. How I am going to approach this is by only finding town. Wolves are playing the master disguise and I won't be able to pinpoint a specific person as being wolfy.

First of all, tikkimatoa, good job my friend. Hard work from day 1, although trailing off a bit. Your purity and innocence gets you a pass on this one. Man, you were even jailkept and I can totally see mafia trying to kill you for your brilliant day 1. Sometimes I disagree with your points, however it is apparent that you are solving from a perspective where you don't know anyone's alignments.

As for benjaminsz, he is also town. This guy had his ups and downs, but he is very proactive, dam. Check out 153, 50% of his posts were defending against attacks, while attacking the weak target. Sheep was such an easy mislynch target he knew he would have a blowout MLing him. In addition his scum game he learned that he should never even try to provoke me when I'm town because he understands that a lot of my perspective comes from an OMGUS (and I'm obviously trying to fix this), having experience with me etc.
Having the huge argument with me a the start of the game is a very risky move to start off the game as a wolf

summitwei is town, but NOT because of his holster argument. I know summit is capable of clearing himself like that as town or wolf. The thing I am really eyeing is his persistence under pressure. Keep in mind, his tikki/jeff read was obviously an interaction read.

hd is uber towny for the wrong reasons, and I will abuse this lopphole of the game. His ISO is 25% trying to derpclear himself, lol. Just accept it XD

Post2. N/A
Post3. Plenty of people ask questions to advance the game. Post 4. Ok, if saying idk is redudant, then it should be NAI, yes? Why should this be scummy, then?

I still think chess’s post is reachy, and at this point it seems to be less about finding me scummy and more about finding me wrong. I provided reasons for why none of these points are actually scummy on my part.

SFGiantsFan looked down from meme heaven.

Hello, tikkitamatoa and chess24 have great potential.

Best newbie game in terms of play quality in a while. Also, nice flavor lol.

One thing I noticed in this and other games (and in my play as well) is that some town get intimidated by wallposts, aggressive tone, etc. and don't want to go against whoever is doing that kind of thing, which lets strong mafia powerwolf their way through. If town lets mafia power through everything they don't stand much of a chance (e.g. this game although town was towny and had solid reads they didn't win because mafia took control of the whole game). Town needs to make sure to interact with each other and establish a good hold on the game (while avoiding complacency and stubbornness of course).
